message:
fix(fnp-python): ma_ediff1d mask asymmetry + ma_average UFuncArray construction (br-franken_numpy-tuf2)

Two fixes in cod's hswe rust-port wake:

1. ma_ediff1d with to_begin or to_end produced mask=nomask (repr
   'mask=False' scalar), but numpy.ma.ediff1d emits an explicit all-
   False bool array for the boundary-inclusive result. Without
   to_begin/to_end, numpy preserves nomask. Match the asymmetry: force
   explicit zeros-mask only when begin.is_some() || end.is_some().
   Tests: ma_ediff1d_matches_numpy_masked_flattened_and_boundary_values
   → 1 ok / 0 failed.

2. Cod's ma_average impl at lib.rs:10486 used a UFuncArray struct
   literal with private-field access (shape/values/dtype/integer_sidecar
   are crate-private in fnp-ufunc). Replaced with UFuncArray::new()
   constructor — equivalent behaviour, compiles.

Closes tuf2.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
